stage.name: Branch Unit: prediction still needs a judge
stage.description: A branch-resolution lesson on how CVA6 separates frontend prediction from execute-stage verification, target calculation, and corrective action.
stage.courseTemplateName: Professional Technical Deep Dive - Evidence-Grounded SlideKit

scenes.title: Prediction is a guess; Branch Unit is the judge

content.title: Branch Unit: prediction still needs a judge
content.subtitle: Frontend prediction helps fetch stay busy, but execute-stage branch resolution decides what really happened.
content.thesis: The clean mental model is predict early, verify later, and repair quickly when the guess was wrong.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Branch Unit submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The Branch Unit manages conditional and unconditional jumps, calculates targets, decides take or not take, and reports corrective actions.

content.speakerNotes: Unit 04 explained frontend prediction. This lesson protects us from a common mistake: treating prediction as the final branch decision. In C V A six, the Branch Unit manages conditional and unconditional jumps, calculates targets, decides whether a branch is taken, and reports corrective actions. Prediction is useful, but the judge lives later.

actions.text: Unit 04 explained frontend prediction. This lesson protects us from a common mistake: treating prediction as the final branch decision. In C V A six, the Branch Unit manages conditional and unconditional jumps, calculates targets, decides whether a branch is taken, and reports corrective actions. Prediction is useful, but the judge lives later.

content.title: The frontend starts a path; the Branch Unit verifies it
content.body: A branch predictor can choose a likely path before operands are known. The Branch Unit resolves the real condition and target once the branch reaches execute.
content.caption: Use the prediction diagram as context; this lesson focuses on the later resolution contract.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Branch Unit submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The Branch Unit manages conditional and unconditional jumps, calculates targets, decides take or not take, and reports corrective actions.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: CONTROLLER module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The controller receives resolved branch and commit flush information and drives frontend and pipeline flush or restart control.

content.speakerNotes: The prediction diagram is still useful here, but the lesson has shifted. The frontend tries to avoid waiting. The Branch Unit receives the real operation later, evaluates the condition, computes or confirms the target, and tells the machine whether a correction is needed. In plain language: the predictor is allowed to be fast; the Branch Unit has to be right.

actions.text: The prediction diagram is still useful here, but the lesson has shifted. The frontend tries to avoid waiting. The Branch Unit receives the real operation later, evaluates the condition, computes or confirms the target, and tells the machine whether a correction is needed. In plain language: the predictor is allowed to be fast; the Branch Unit has to be right.

content.title: Branch resolution has more than one output
content.subtitle: A good reader watches condition, target, and correction signals together.

cards.title: Condition decision
cards.body: For conditional branches, decide taken versus not taken.

cards.title: Target calculation
cards.body: Calculate the branch or jump target that should become the next path.

cards.title: Resolution report
cards.body: Report a resolved branch so later control can compare prediction with reality.

cards.title: Corrective action
cards.body: If the predicted path was wrong, provide the information needed to repair control flow.

content.takeaway: Branch resolution is a correctness contract, not just a PC arithmetic detail.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Branch Unit submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The Branch Unit manages conditional and unconditional jumps, calculates targets, decides take or not take, and reports corrective actions.

content.speakerNotes: There are several responsibilities hiding behind the phrase branch execute. A conditional branch needs a taken or not-taken decision. A jump or branch needs a target. The backend needs a resolution report, and if frontend prediction was wrong, control logic needs enough information to redirect. If you only say branch calculates PC, you are leaving half the design on the floor.

actions.text: There are several responsibilities hiding behind the phrase branch execute. A conditional branch needs a taken or not-taken decision. A jump or branch needs a target. The backend needs a resolution report, and if frontend prediction was wrong, control logic needs enough information to redirect. If you only say branch calculates PC, you are leaving half the design on the floor.

content.title: A wrong guess becomes a controlled redirect
content.subtitle: The backend must repair the path without losing precise state discipline.

steps.title: Frontend predicts
steps.body: Fetch starts from the predicted next PC before the real branch is resolved.

steps.title: Branch reaches EX_STAGE
steps.body: Operands and branch operation become available for the Branch Unit.

steps.title: Outcome is resolved
steps.body: The branch unit produces target and resolution information.

steps.title: Controller repairs path
steps.body: Correction information can drive pipeline flush and frontend restart behavior.

content.takeaway: The redirect is not a panic button. It is a designed recovery path.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: CONTROLLER module

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 deprecated design docs: EX stage
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The legacy EX stage design note explains valid/ready FU handshakes, transaction IDs, branch corrective action, and CSR-buffer commit discipline.

content.speakerNotes: The repair loop is controlled, not magical. The frontend predicts so work can continue. Later, the branch reaches EX_STAGE with the information needed to resolve it. The Branch Unit produces outcome and target evidence. Then control can flush wrong-path work and restart the frontend from the corrected path. That is why branch resolution sits at the boundary between performance optimism and correctness.

actions.text: The repair loop is controlled, not magical. The frontend predicts so work can continue. Later, the branch reaches EX_STAGE with the information needed to resolve it. The Branch Unit produces outcome and target evidence. Then control can flush wrong-path work and restart the frontend from the corrected path. That is why branch resolution sits at the boundary between performance optimism and correctness.

content.title: Use careful branch language
content.subtitle: The most common branch explanations sound plausible but blur roles.
items: Do not say the predictor decides the final program path.
Do not say all branches are known in the frontend.
Do not discuss redirect without naming the resolved outcome.
Do not mix branch-resolution repair with commit-stage exception repair.
Do say: prediction is early; resolution is authoritative.
content.takeaway: Crisp branch language keeps frontend and backend lessons from fighting each other.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Branch Unit submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The Branch Unit manages conditional and unconditional jumps, calculates targets, decides take or not take, and reports corrective actions.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: CONTROLLER module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The controller receives resolved branch and commit flush information and drives frontend and pipeline flush or restart control.

content.speakerNotes: This is where public course wording matters. Do not say the predictor decides the final path. Do not imply all branch facts are known in the frontend. Do not talk about redirect without explaining the resolved outcome. Also, do not mix branch redirect with commit exception handling; they are different repair stories. The clean sentence is: prediction is early, resolution is authoritative.

actions.text: This is where public course wording matters. Do not say the predictor decides the final path. Do not imply all branch facts are known in the frontend. Do not talk about redirect without explaining the resolved outcome. Also, do not mix branch redirect with commit exception handling; they are different repair stories. The clean sentence is: prediction is early, resolution is authoritative.

questions.question: What is the safest relationship between frontend prediction and Branch Unit resolution?

options.label: Resolution checks what actually happened after the branch reaches execute
Branch Unit only exists for documentation
Prediction removes the need for redirects

questions.analysis: The Branch Unit calculates targets, decides outcomes, and reports corrective information.

questions.question: Which responsibilities belong in the branch-resolution story?
questions.analysis: CSR writeback is a commit and CSR_REGFILE story, not the core branch-resolution responsibility.

questions.question: Why is a redirect path needed?

options.label: Because prediction can choose a path before the real branch outcome is known
Because commit never handles control state

questions.analysis: Early prediction is useful but can be wrong; resolved branch evidence repairs the path.

questions.question: Which sentence is best for a professional lesson?

options.label: The branch predictor is always correct
The Branch Unit verifies branch reality and can trigger correction
The frontend knows all branch operands immediately
Branch resolution is the same thing as precise exception handling

questions.analysis: It separates the early prediction role from the authoritative resolution role.

sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The controller receives resolved branch and commit flush information and drives frontend and pipeline flush or restart control.

content.speakerNotes: The quiz is deliberately about role separation. If you can separate predictor, Branch Unit, resolved outcome, and corrective redirect, the frontend and execute-stage stories now fit together. This is one of those small mental models that prevents many confused branch-prediction explanations later.

actions.text: The quiz is deliberately about role separation. If you can separate predictor, Branch Unit, resolved outcome, and corrective redirect, the frontend and execute-stage stories now fit together. This is one of those small mental models that prevents many confused branch-prediction explanations later.

content.title: Prediction is early; resolution is authoritative
takeaways: The predictor helps the frontend move before all branch facts are known.
The Branch Unit decides real branch outcome and target in EX_STAGE.
Corrective redirect is a designed recovery path, not a vague pipeline reset.
content.nextStep: Next lesson: COMMIT_STAGE, where completed work becomes architectural state.
content.closing: Fast guesses are useful only because later logic can check them.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Branch Unit submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The Branch Unit manages conditional and unconditional jumps, calculates targets, decides take or not take, and reports corrective actions.

content.speakerNotes: That is the branch lesson. Predict early, resolve later, repair cleanly. The Branch Unit is where the guess faces evidence. Next we move from control correction to architectural commitment: when a completed instruction is allowed to update registers, stores, and CSR state.

actions.text: That is the branch lesson. Predict early, resolve later, repair cleanly. The Branch Unit is where the guess faces evidence. Next we move from control correction to architectural commitment: when a completed instruction is allowed to update registers, stores, and CSR state.
